products.name: Blueberries
products.hts_description: Cranberries, blueberries, and other fruits of the genus Vaccinium, fresh
products.duty_rate: Free (varies by 10-digit subheading — confirm in HTSUS)
products.duty_notes: Many lines duty-free under Column 1 General. Section 301 tariffs may apply depending on country of origin.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Required for most fresh fruit imports. Must be issued by national plant protection organization of exporting country.
All fresh produce subject to inspection upon arrival at US port of entry.
Must be submitted before shipment arrives in the US. Submit via FDA Prior Notice System Interface (PNSI).
Required for all commercial food import shipments. Must renew every two years.

products.systems_approach: Fresh blueberries may require a Systems Approach protocol depending on origin country and pest risk. Check USDA APHIS PPQ import requirements for specific origin.

products.name: Asparagus

hts_subheadings.notes: Asparagus entered under specific seasonal conditions

products.duty_notes: Duty rates vary by season of entry and shipment conditions. Confirm exact rate for specific subheading and entry date in HTSUS.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Commonly required for fresh asparagus imports. Must accompany shipment.
Subject to plant health inspection at port of entry.
Mandatory for all food imports into the US.
Required for commercial shipments.

products.systems_approach: Fresh asparagus is a regulated plant product under USDA APHIS. Origin-specific conditions apply.

products.name: Grapes

hts_subheadings.notes: Grapes entered during specific seasonal window

products.duty_rate: Seasonal (some lines duty-free, others carry tariffs)
products.duty_notes: Classification is highly seasonal. Duty rate depends on date of entry. Confirm exact subheading and rate in HTSUS before shipment.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Fresh grapes are regulated agricultural commodities. Phytosanitary certification often required depending on origin.
Inspection required at US port of entry.
Mandatory prior notice before arrival.
Required for all commercial food imports.

products.systems_approach: Fresh grapes may require Systems Approach protocols or cold treatment depending on origin. Verify with USDA APHIS PPQ for specific country.

products.name: Coffee (Green / Unroasted)
products.duty_notes: Enters duty-free under Column 1 General. No additional tariffs typically apply.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Mandatory for all food imports.
Required for commercial shipments.
Green coffee is generally authorized as a non-propagative plant product. No APHIS permit typically required but confirm based on origin.

products.systems_approach: Green coffee beans are generally not subject to Systems Approach protocols. Treated as non-propagative commodity.

products.name: Coffee (Roasted)

permits_and_requirements.notes: Mandatory for all food imports.
Required for commercial shipments.
Roasting process means APHIS permit is not required.

products.systems_approach: Not applicable. Roasting eliminates plant health concerns.

products.name: Quinoa
products.duty_notes: US General duty rate is 1.1% ad valorem. Country of origin may affect rate — verify current HTSUS listing.

permits_and_requirements.notes: Mandatory for all food imports.
Required for commercial shipments.
Quinoa is typically considered a non-propagative grain for human consumption. Permit requirements depend on origin and any seed treatment applied.

products.systems_approach: Food grain quinoa not subject to Systems Approach. Seed import rules only apply if quinoa is imported for planting purposes.

universal_compliance_notes: FDA Prior Notice is mandatory for every food shipment entering the US
FDA Food Facility Registration required for all commercial food imports
USDA APHIS PPQ regulates all fresh produce and some grain imports
Country of origin may trigger additional Section 301 tariffs
Always confirm the exact 10-digit HTS code before filing entry
Phytosanitary requirements vary by country of origin
Systems Approach protocols vary by product and origin — verify with USDA APHIS
